metaTitle: Recovering from Surgery? Post-Op Bracing & Cold Therapy | Avana Surgical
metaDescription: Doctor-recommended post-surgical bracing, cold therapy and recovery support for knee, shoulder, hip and spine surgery patients. Fitted in India.

hero.title: Recover safely. Get back to life faster.
hero.description: The first 6–12 weeks after surgery shape your long-term recovery. The right post-op support — bracing, cold therapy, and motion control — can reduce pain, prevent setbacks, and accelerate return to normal life.

conditionsHeading.title: What does post-op support look like?
conditionsHeading.lead: Different surgeries need different support — here's how we help.

conditions.title: Pain and swelling control
conditions.description: Cold therapy with active compression dramatically reduces post-op pain and inflammation — often cutting medication needs.

conditions.title: Controlled immobilisation
conditions.description: Many surgeries — shoulder, hip, knee — need the joint protected from harmful motion while it heals.

conditions.title: Range-of-motion progression
conditions.description: Hinged bracing lets your surgeon precisely control how much your joint moves at each stage of recovery.

conditions.title: Safe return to walking
conditions.description: Functional bracing and walkers help you weight-bear safely while bone, ligament, or tendon healing completes.

productsHeading.title: Recovery solutions trusted by surgeons
productsHeading.lead: These products are widely used in Indian operating theatres and post-op clinics — the same support your surgeon may have recommended.

testimonialsHeading.title: Patients who recovered with our support

testimonials.name: Walid Abedin
testimonials.testimonial: I had ACL reconstruction and the pain was unimaginable — easily a 9 out of 10. The combination of cold therapy and compression in this product is game changing. Pain reduced after just two uses.

testimonials.name: Senthil

seoContent.title: The science of good post-op recovery
seoContent.intro: Surgery is one half of getting better. The other half is the recovery plan — and the equipment that supports it.

sections.heading: Why post-op pain control matters
sections.body: Poorly controlled post-op pain delays mobilisation, increases medication side effects, and is one of the strongest predictors of slow recovery. Cold therapy with active compression has been shown to reduce post-operative pain medication needs and speed return-to-function.

sections.heading: Bracing protects what your surgeon fixed
sections.body: Whether it's an ACL graft, a rotator cuff repair, or a new hip joint, the surgical fix is fragile in the early weeks. A correctly-prescribed brace keeps the joint within safe limits while it heals — avoiding setbacks that can mean re-operation.

sections.heading: Follow your surgeon's protocol
sections.body: Brace type, wear schedule, and motion limits should be guided by your operating surgeon. Our specialists work with your surgeon's instructions, not around them.

seoContent.disclaimer: Educational content. Always follow your surgeon's specific recovery instructions.

faq.question: How long do I need to wear a post-op brace?
faq.answer: It depends entirely on the procedure and your surgeon's protocol. Most post-op braces are worn continuously for 4–6 weeks then weaned over the next 6–12 weeks. Always follow your surgeon's specific instructions.

faq.question: Is cold therapy better than ice packs?
faq.answer: For most post-op situations, yes. Powered cold therapy with active compression maintains a more consistent temperature, provides circulating compression that reduces swelling, and doesn't require constant refreshing like ice packs do.

faq.question: Can I shower with my brace on?
faq.answer: Most rigid post-op braces should be removed for showering then replaced immediately. Your surgeon will advise based on your specific situation — some early-stage protocols require continuous wear.
